Guffa Review - Empires & Puzzles Hero Strategy Guide

Is Guffa the ultimate Rush Attack tank or just a slow moving statue? We break down this massive Gargoyle's unique kit, featuring Taunt, Softskin for allies, and a massive delayed heal that can turn the tide of battle.
Guffa, The Stoic Gargoyle: The Red Wall of Silence

Guffa is a Legendary Fire hero from the Gargoyle family, released back in mid-2024. He represents the classic "immovable object" archetype—a massive, slow Paladin designed to absorb punishment while his team prepares to strike. His kit is a complex mix of Taunt, Softskin, and a delayed but massive heal, making him a specialized tool rather than a general-purpose warrior. While he may feel a bit sluggish in standard play, he has found a permanent home in the Rush Attack niche. Just don't expect him to win any sprints.

Guffa is arguably the "fluffiest" of the Gargoyles, sporting a vibrant red coat and a massive axe that looks like it weighs more than most 3-star heroes. The art leans into the "gentle giant" trope until you realize that giant is a demon holding a weapon of mass destruction. He stands out from the greyer, stonier aesthetic of his Gargoyle brethren with a fiery, almost playful color palette that belies his defensive nature.

When Guffa was released in 2024, his 1080 power was impressive. However, in the current meta of late 2025/early 2026, where new Legendary heroes regularly push past 1250+ power, Guffa's base stats show his age. His defense and health are still respectable enough to let him do his job as a tank, especially with the Paladin class, but he lacks the sheer stat-stick dominance of modern releases. He relies heavily on his damage reduction mechanics to survive, rather than raw numbers.

Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The Gargoyle family is famous for its Stoneskin passive, and Guffa is no exception.
Immunity to Poison, Burn, and Water damage adds another layer of survivability, especially against common DOT-heavy defenses.
is his personal safety net. Even if his Taunt is dispelled, he personally takes -90% damage for the first few hits after firing. This makes him incredibly annoying to finish off.

fits his kit perfectly. Since he is a Taunter, he will be taking a lot of hits. Reducing that incoming damage by a flat percentage (usually 5-10% depending on level) stacks well with his high defense.
Upon release, Guffa was seen as a "Rush War specialist." Players debated if his slow speed made him "trash" or "OP." Time has proven that in Rush formats, he is a monster.
His stats have fallen behind. A 1080 Power hero is now comparable to a highly emblemmed 4-star in terms of raw stats, but his
(90% reduction, flat 1400 heal) are percentage/fixed value based and do not age as poorly as direct damage dealers.

If you pull Guffa early, he will carry you through difficult map stages and quests. His survivability is unmatched for a new roster. However, do not prioritize him over a versatile fast sniper for Raids. Level him when you need a tank for Rush wars.

Guffa is a luxury tool for your Rush War depth. He doesn't need Alpha Aethers to do his job (Taunt is Taunt), but the extra stats help. If you have modern taunters like Phorcys or reliable fast tanks, Guffa might ride the bench. However, his "Softskin for Allies" is unique enough to warrant keeping him for specific counter-play.
- A prophetic forum user predicting his dominance in fast mana modes.
"Slow mana but... stats allow to survive."
- This was true in 2024, but in 2026, those stats are less reliable without Limit Breaks.
Community consensus generally settled on him being "Broken in Rush, Average elsewhere."

Guffa is a relic of a slightly different era—one where Slow heroes were allowed to be impactful without destroying the entire board in one move. He is a specialized tool, a safety valve that you open when the game rules (Rush Attack) favor the heavyweights. While he won't be your everyday hero, on those specific weeks, you'll be glad you have the big red gargoyle on your side.
No costume? No problem. He's already wearing enough stone.
Keep and max for Rush Attack. Bench for standard play.
